Definition (EN): a definition element classifies a certain kind of element (e.g., a classification of attributes, parts, actions, etc.).
Context/Example (EN): In general, a definition element classifies a certain kind of element (e.g., a classification of attributes, parts, actions, etc.).

Definition (EN): A usage element is a usage of a definition element in a certain context.
Context/Example (EN): A usage element is a usage of a definition element in a certain context.

Definition (EN): A definition may have owned usage elements nested in it, referred to as its features.
Context/Example (EN): A definition may have owned usage elements nested in it, referred to as its features.

Definition (EN): The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.

Equivalent (JA): バリエーションポイント
Definition (EN): A variation (sometimes referred to as a variation point) identifies an element in a model that can vary from one design configuration to another.
Context/Example (EN): A variation (sometimes referred to as a variation point) identifies an element in a model that can vary from one design configuration to another.

Definition (EN): For each variation, there are design choices called variants.
Context/Example (EN): For each variation, there are design choices called variants.

Definition (EN): Attributes that specify characteristics of something that can be defined by simple or compound data types, and dimensional quantities such as mass, length, etc.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of attributive information about things, including: Attributes that specify characteristics of something that can be defined by simple or compound data types, and dimensional quantities such as mass, length, etc. (see 7.7 ).

Definition (EN): Enumerations that are attributes restricted to a specified set of enumerated values.
Context/Example (EN): Enumerations that are attributes restricted to a specified set of enumerated values (see 7.8 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of occurrences with temporal and spatial extent.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of occurrences with temporal and spatial extent. Temporal extent enables an occurrence to be represented at specific points in time, over a duration in time, or over an entire lifetime. Spatial extent enables an occurrence to be represented at a position and orientation with respect to a coordinate frame, and to have a shape and size (see 7.9 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of individuals with specific identities.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of individuals with specific identities (see 7.9 ).

Definition (EN): A definition element classifies a certain kind of element (e.g., a classification of attributes, parts, actions, etc.).
Context/Example (EN): A definition is declared using a keyword specific to the kind of definition (e.g., item, part, action, etc.) followed by the keyword def.

Definition (EN): A definition is specified as abstract by placing the keyword abstract before its kind keyword.
Context/Example (EN): A definition is specified as abstract by placing the keyword abstract before its kind keyword.

Definition (EN): A definition that is not abstract is called a concrete definition.
Context/Example (EN): A definition that is not abstract is called a concrete definition.

Definition (EN): A usage is declared using a keyword specific to the kind of usage (e.g., item, part, action, etc.).
Context/Example (EN): A usage is declared using a keyword specific to the kind of usage (e.g., item, part, action, etc.).

Equivalent (JA): フィーチャタイピング
Definition (EN): Feature typings specify the definitions of a usage (also known as its types).
Context/Example (EN): Feature typings specify the definitions of a usage (also known as its types).

Definition (EN): Subsettings specify other usages subsetted by the owning usage.
Context/Example (EN): Subsettings specify other usages subsetted by the owning usage.

Definition (EN): Redefinitions specify other usages redefined by the owning usage.
Context/Example (EN): Redefinitions specify other usages redefined by the owning usage.

Definition (EN): A usage may have a multiplicity that constrains its cardinality, that is, the allowed number of values it may have for any instance of its featuring definition or usage.
Context/Example (EN): A usage may have a multiplicity that constrains its cardinality, that is, the allowed number of values it may have for any instance of its featuring definition or usage.

Definition (EN): A multiplicity range is written in the form [lowerBound..upperBound], where each of lowerBound and upperBound is either a literal or the identification of a usage.
Context/Example (EN): A multiplicity range is written in the form [lowerBound..upperBound], where each of lowerBound and upperBound is either a literal or the identification of a usage.

Definition (EN): If a usage is non-unique, then the same value may appear more than once as a value of the usage.
Context/Example (EN): nonunique – If a usage is non-unique, then the same value may appear more than once as a value of the usage.

Definition (EN): If a usage is ordered, then the values of the usage can be placed in order, indexed from 1 to the number of values.
Context/Example (EN): ordered – If a usage is ordered, then the values of the usage can be placed in order, indexed from 1 to the number of values.

Definition (EN): A referential usage represents a simple reference between a featuring instance and one or more values.
Context/Example (EN): A usage may be referential or composite. A referential usage represents a simple reference between a featuring instance and one or more values.

Definition (EN): A composite usage, on the other hand, indicates that the related instance is integral to the structure of the containing instance.
Context/Example (EN): A composite usage, on the other hand, indicates that the related instance is integral to the structure of the containing instance.

Definition (EN): Specifies that the usage is derived.
Context/Example (EN): derived – Specifies that the usage is derived.

Definition (EN): Specifies that the usage is abstract. A usage that is not abstract is called a concrete usage.
Context/Example (EN): abstract – Specifies that the usage is abstract.

Definition (EN): Specifies that the usage is constant. Values of a constant usage are the same during the entire existence of the featuring instance.
Context/Example (EN): constant – Specifies that the usage is constant.

Definition (EN): Specifies that the usage is referential.
Context/Example (EN): ref – Specifies that the usage is referential.

Definition (EN): Things "outside" the featuring instance. These usages identify things input to an instance.
Context/Example (EN): in – Things "outside" the featuring instance. These usages identify things input to an instance.

Definition (EN): The featuring instance itself or things "inside" it. These usages identify things output by an instance.
Context/Example (EN): out – The featuring instance itself or things "inside" it. These usages identify things output by an instance.

Definition (EN): Both things "outside" and "inside" the featuring instance. These usages identify things that are both input to and output by an instance.
Context/Example (EN): inout – Both things "outside" and "inside" the featuring instance. These usages identify things that are both input to and output by an instance.

Definition (EN): A reference usage is a usage that is declared without any kind keyword. Unlike other kinds of usages, the definitions (types) of a reference usage are not restricted to be of a particular kind.
Context/Example (EN): A reference usage is a usage that is declared without any kind keyword.

Definition (EN): If a name and/or short name are declared for an element, these are used in the name resolution process to identify the element (as discussed in 7.6.1 ). However, if neither a name or a short name are given in the declaration of a usage with an owned redefinition, then its effective name and short name are implicitly determined by the name and short name of the redefining usage of its first owned redefinition (which may itself be an implicit name, if the redefined usage is itself a redefining usage).
Context/Example (EN): If a name and/or short name are declared for an element, these are used in the name resolution process to identify the element (as discussed in 7.6.1 ).

Definition (EN): A feature chain is a textual notation specified as a sequence of two or more qualified names separated by dot (.) symbols.
Context/Example (EN): A feature chain is a textual notation specified as a sequence of two or more qualified names separated by dot (.) symbols.

Definition (EN): A definition or usage is specified as a variation by placing the keyword variation before its kind keyword.
Context/Example (EN): A definition or usage is specified as a variation by placing the keyword variation before its kind keyword.

Definition (EN): All usages declared within the body of a variation definition or usage are declared as variant usages by placing the keyword variant at the beginning of their declarations.
Context/Example (EN): All usages declared within the body of a variation definition or usage are declared as variant usages by placing the keyword variant at the beginning of their declarations.

Definition (EN): if a definition or usage, as explicitly declared, does not directly or indirectly specialize the required base definition or usage, then the declaration is considered to include an implicit subclassification or subsetting of the appropriate base definition or usage.
Context/Example (EN): if a definition or usage, as explicitly declared, does not directly or indirectly specialize the required base definition or usage, then the declaration is considered to include an implicit subclassification or subsetting of the appropriate base definition or usage.
